nativeRange.habitat: Moist lowland regions, riverbanks and periodically flooded areas; tolerant of slightly brackish conditions in coastal wetlands

decorPreferences: bright overhead lighting to keep growth compact

summary: A short, grass-like South American carpet plant that spreads by runners to form a dense foreground lawn — similar in look to Dwarf Sagittaria but markedly shorter, generally staying under about 7 cm.
careNotes: Lilaeopsis brasiliensis is widely sold under the trade names 'Micro Sword' and 'Brazilian Micro Sword'. It is a true foreground carpet plant, typically reaching only about 3–7 cm tall — noticeably shorter than its frequent comparison plant Sagittaria subulata, which tends toward the midground. Aquarium Co-Op describes it as 'usually does not get to be more than a few inches tall', and Wikipedia and aqua-fish.net both bracket adult height at roughly 4–7 cm. Lighting demand is generally considered medium-to-high: dimmer setups can still keep the plant alive but tend to produce sparse, leggy growth rather than a true carpet. CO2 is not strictly required but is widely reported to accelerate carpeting and densify runners; targets around 20–30 ppm are commonly cited. The plant is a strong root feeder — a nutrient-rich substrate and regular root tabs are the most-mentioned fertilization advice across sources. Temperature tolerance is reported across a fairly wide band: aqua-fish.net gives 15–24 °C reflecting the cooler subtropical end of its Brazilian range, while most hobby keepers run tanks at 22–26 °C without issue; 20–26 °C is a reasonable consensus. pH 6.5–7.5 and gH 4–18 are well tolerated. Propagation is by horizontal stolons (runners) that send up new plantlets at short intervals — carpet formation from a healthy starter portion typically takes 2–3 months under good conditions. Only the roots should be buried at planting; burying the crown is a common cause of melt. Native to South America (Brazil and neighbouring countries) along riverbanks and seasonally flooded lowlands; mild salinity tolerance has been reported but it is kept strictly as a freshwater plant in the hobby. Care level is rated intermediate rather than beginner because, although it is forgiving once established, achieving a true dense carpet (rather than a thin patchy spread) generally rewards stronger lighting, CO2, and patience — without those, beginners often see disappointing results.
breedingNotes: Propagates very readily by horizontal stolons (runners) from the base of each rosette; daughter plantlets root themselves into the substrate at short intervals and can be cut free and replanted to fill open foreground space. Flowering is occasionally reported on emersed plants but is not a meaningful aquarium propagation route.

primary.name: Aquarium Co-Op
primary.notes: Effective primary after the dispatched Buce Plant product page (buceplant.com/products/lilaeopsis-brasiliensis-tissue-culture) returned HTTP 404. Confirms scientific name Lilaeopsis brasiliensis, common name 'Micro Sword', grass-like foreground/carpet habit, height 'a few inches', spreads via substrate runners forming baby plants, medium-to-higher lighting for carpet formation, monthly root tabs recommended, can grow relatively quickly once established.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Lilaeopsis brasiliensis
additional.notes: Taxonomy fallback (family Apiaceae, order Apiales). Confirms South American origin, height 4–7 cm (1.5–3 in), foreground placement, slow growth, runner propagation, benefits from bright lighting and CO2, nutrient-rich substrate, and notes tolerance of some salinity in water.

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net — Lilaeopsis brasiliensis
additional.notes: Used to fill quantitative water parameters where Aquarium Co-Op was silent: temperature 15–24 °C, pH 6.5–7.5, hardness 4–18 °dGH, size 0–7 cm, slow growth, CO2 20–30 ppm beneficial, 8–10 h bright lighting, runner propagation, 2–3 months to carpet, native to Brazil along riverbanks and flooded areas. Cross-source temperature spread: aqua-fish 15–24 °C vs hobby-typical 22–26 °C; averaged to 20–26 °C as the consensus aquarium-keeping range.

additional.name: Buce Plant (dispatched primary, unreachable)
additional.notes: Dispatched as primary source for this species but returned HTTP 404 on fetch; abandoned per playbook's single-failure rule. Roles swapped: Aquarium Co-Op promoted to primary, Wikipedia and Aqua-Fish.net used as additional cross-verification sources.
